block_02: Effective and converting GEOs are the key to success in our business. Staying on top of the latest changes and using them to their max effectiveness is vital. Step up your game in 2023!
block_03: Finding a golden GEO is not that easy, we know. Let us help you a little! We would like to share some of the hottest GEOs which will kick-start your work.
block_05: The biggest country in Latin America with a 212+ million population, Brazil is bound to bring some big conversions. After successfully operating this GEO for a few years, we know that its traders have a high demand for Binary Options trading – they love it! Tropical Brazil is certainly a fruitful source of traffic.
block_07: With 90% of all internet users accessing the internet through a mobile device, Thailand promises an increase in mobile conversions. Good news – we have just added a new mobile landing! On the new mobile landing page, we replaced the app store download button with a registration form (that boosts conversions to registration). Overall, Thailand remains one of the leading traffic sources in South-East Asia and continues to remain a flourishing GEO.
block_10: A constantly growing netizen population in Colombia is usually characterized by its high engagement rate. With 93% of the population owning a smartphone, it remains the most popular form of access. As mobile data is used the majority of the time, and the mobile internet speeds are not the fastest, using video creatives might not be the best action plan. Try using our
block_12: with 2 screens and see the result for yourself!
block_14: Another popular Latin American GEO is Mexico. 99% of Mexicans use Google and Facebook. As with other LatAm regions, smartphones are the most popular method of digital world entry and consequently, a mobile user remains the most popular internet resident in “El Tri”.
block_16: Being the largest country in the Middle East, the resplendent, oil-rich Saudi Arabia, is known for its traditionalism and strictness – heavily influenced by religion. When working with this GEO, for a most effective result, we advise to not forget about the culture-related traditionalism in your creatives. Try using the local language as well as simple, straight-to-the-point messages (around 6-7 words), numbers and statistics. Make sure to keep an eye out for local holidays and weekends, as customer activity is significantly lower during those times.
block_18: Luxurious. Opulent. Traditional. These are the factors you need to consider when tailoring your promotional content. Being exceptionally developed, the UAE features the largest number of smartphone and internet users in the world. You can smell the opportunities already, right? Do keep in mind that the UAE is a Muslim country, with a high level of traditional values and respect for religion. It is always advised to use local dialects and present offers promoting quality
block_24: Indonesia is a relatively new, up and coming source of traffic. With around only half of the population being active users of the web (almost 150 million), the main target falls on the younger demographic. Be sure to analyze your target audience, primarily taking the age demographic into account. Feel free to experiment with your approaches and find the most effective way for you!
block_26: In a constant race for the number one spot of the country with the largest world population, India has a enormous potential quantity of convertible leads. With a lot of room for improvisation, feel free to use the classic and simple approaches to advertising – flashy landings and words like “free” (e.g. demo account). Keep in mind that while the national currency is Rupee, advertising in Dollars may also yield a result.
block_28: As with all GEOs, you have to know the mentality of your audience before you start. Malaysia is a monarchy where most of the population is Islamic. Therefore, it is not recommended to engage in aggressive advertising and try to have it culturally tailored as much as possible. With the local language being prevalent, advertising should be in Malay too. This GEO has been recognised as a country with one of the worst internet speeds in the world, so most of the traffic will be mobile - keep that in mind.
block_29: If you need any tips and assistance on working with these GEOs, do not hesitate to message your personal manager.
